meaning: adj.烤焦的,枯萎的vt.烤焦,使枯萎vi.凋谢,干枯
examples: I distinctly felt the heat start to sear my throat.
Several direct hits of lightning to the Sear tower were documented.
It seemed that he began to sear.
It would be similar to taking advice on how to pan-sear steak from a ten-year vegan.
These startups hoped that Super Bowl exposure would sear their web address into the minds of consumers.
